steps.title: Match both parts of the claim
steps.content: The quotation must show Winnemucca directly addressing the reader and establishing her own authority or credibility. A quotation that only addresses the reader is not enough.

steps.title: Reject partial matches
steps.content: Choice A addresses the reader but only invites imagination. Choice B explains a cultural term without direct reader address or an explicit credibility claim. Choice C addresses the reader and explains a custom, but it does not assert Winnemucca's authority. The remaining quote directly addresses the reader and says she knows what she is talking about. The answer is D.
